skill.name: seo-authority-builder
skill.description: Audits SEO content for E-E-A-T signals, credibility gaps, author authority, trust elements, and topical coverage improvements.
skill.summary: Builds practical E-E-A-T improvement plans for SEO content authority and trust.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported Markdown backticks, description text, and checklist wording as possible security issues. Review found no executable code, network calls, weak cryptography, prompt injection, or data exfiltration behavior in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Inline Backticks
low_findings.description: The reported external command pattern is Markdown formatting around resources/implementation-playbook.md. It is an instruction to open a reference file, not Ruby or shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 25 contains a Markdown inline code span around a file path. No executable syntax, command interpolation, or shell invocation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fence
low_findings.description: The reported backtick execution pattern is a fenced example block that shows an E-E-A-T plan format. It contains plain text checklist content, not executable commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks open and close a Markdown code fence for display. The enclosed lines are template text and numbered recommendations, not runnable code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography hit is on the YAML description for the skill. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing operation, or security-sensitive transformation appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is descriptive metadata about E-E-A-T analysis. There is no code and no mention of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak algorithms.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reported network reconnaissance hit is a trust checklist item about a fact-checking process. It does not request scanning, probing, enumeration, or network access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 118 is normal editorial process language. No hostnames, ports, protocols, scanning tools, or network commands are present.

content.user_title: Build E-E-A-T Authority for SEO Content
content.value_statement: YMYL and expert content need clear trust signals to earn reader confidence. This skill audits E-E-A-T gaps and turns them into practical improvement plans.
actual_capabilities: Scores content against experience, expertise, authority, and trust signals.
Identifies missing author credentials, citations, policies, and proof points.
Recommends trust elements such as editorial policies, contact details, and reviews.
Maps topical authority gaps and internal linking opportunities.
Suggests schema and content additions for credibility-focused pages.
limitations: It provides guidance only and does not verify live search rankings.
It does not replace legal, medical, or financial expert review for YMYL content.
It cannot confirm credentials or citations unless the user provides evidence.
The referenced implementation playbook is not included in this package.

use_cases.title: Improve YMYL Article Trust
use_cases.description: Review sensitive content for missing citations, author credentials, update policies, and reader trust signals.

use_cases.title: Build Expert Author Pages
use_cases.description: Create stronger author bio requirements, credential displays, and proof points for a content team.

use_cases.title: Plan Topical Authority
use_cases.description: Find gaps in topic coverage and define supporting content that strengthens expertise around a subject.

prompt_templates.title: Basic E-E-A-T Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this page for E-E-A-T signals. Score experience, expertise, authority, and trust from 1 to 10. List the top five improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Author Credibility Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these author details and page excerpts. Recommend credentials, proof points, disclosures, and bio changes that would improve trust.

prompt_templates.title: YMYL Trust Upgrade
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this YMYL content for trust risks. Identify missing citations, review processes, policies, warnings, and expert validation needs.

prompt_templates.title: Authority Roadmap
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a 90-day authority roadmap for this topic cluster. Include content gaps, internal links, citation targets, schema ideas, and credibility assets.

output_examples.input: A health advice article with a short byline and no cited sources.
output: Add reviewer credentials, cite current medical sources, and show the update date.
Add an editorial policy and clarify when readers should contact a licensed professional.

output_examples.input: A B2B software comparison page with customer claims and no proof.
output: Primary gap: trust claims are unsupported.
Add customer examples, third-party review references, methodology notes, and author expertise.
Create supporting pages for security, pricing transparency, and implementation experience.

output_examples.input: A topic cluster for retirement planning content.
output: Build author pages for financial experts and disclose review standards.
Create pages for risk, taxes, withdrawals, and account types to improve topical depth.
Use citations from official agencies and recognized financial education sources.

best_practices: Provide the target page, audience, topic sensitivity, and any available author credentials.
Ask for prioritized actions when implementation time or budget is limited.
Verify recommended claims, citations, and credentials before publishing changes.
anti_patterns: Do not add fake credentials, invented reviews, or unsupported authority claims.
Do not treat E-E-A-T recommendations as guaranteed ranking improvements.
Do not use generic trust badges without explaining what they prove.

faq.question: What does this skill audit?
faq.answer: It reviews content for experience, expertise, authority, and trust signals that support SEO credibility.

faq.question: Can it improve YMYL pages?
faq.answer: Yes. It can identify trust gaps, but qualified experts should review sensitive legal, medical, or financial advice.

faq.question: Does it guarantee higher rankings?
faq.answer: No. It improves content quality signals, but search rankings depend on many external factors.

faq.question: What inputs work best?
faq.answer: Provide page text, author details, citations, business context, and the target audience.

faq.question: Can it create schema recommendations?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest relevant organization, author, article, and topical schema ideas.

faq.question: Is it compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
